description: src/projects.json exists after running the migration script
steps: Run src/projects-migrate
Assert src/projects.json exists

description: all projects from projects-list.zsh are present in src/projects.json with correct top-level keys sorted alphabetically
steps: Run src/projects-migrate
Assert every project name from the PROJECTS associative array in src/projects-list.zsh appears as a top-level key in src/projects.json
Assert top-level keys are in alphabetical order

description: each project object contains background, foreground, icon fields matching projects-list.zsh source values
steps: Run src/projects-migrate
Assert a project with background/foreground/icon in projects-list.zsh has those fields in src/projects.json as semantic color names (e.g. 'YELLOW_7')
Assert path uses tilde notation with no trailing slash

description: hideNameInPrompt is present and true only for projects with hideNameInPrompt:1 in source
steps: Run src/projects-migrate
Assert projects with hideNameInPrompt=1 in projects-list.zsh have hideNameInPrompt:true in src/projects.json
Assert projects without hideNameInPrompt do not have the key in src/projects.json

description: projects with only an icon (no path, no colors) are included with just the icon key
steps: Run src/projects-migrate
Find a project in projects-list.zsh that has only an icon set
Assert its entry in src/projects.json contains only the 'icon' key

description: minimal project with background, foreground, icon, path produces correct name/ansi/hex in dist JSON
steps: Create a minimal src/projects.json with one project: background 'GREEN_8', foreground 'GRAY_9', icon and path set
Create a minimal colors mock that defines COLOR_GREEN_8, COLOR_GREEN_8_HEXA, COLOR_GRAY_9, COLOR_GRAY_9_HEXA, COLOR_DARK_GREEN, COLOR_DARK_GREEN_HEXA
Run src/projects-build
Assert dist/projects.json contains background.name='GREEN_8', background.ansi=correct integer, background.hex=correct hex string
Assert dist/projects.json contains foreground.name='GRAY_9', foreground.ansi and foreground.hex

description: background with numeric suffix derives backgroundInactive by stripping suffix and prepending DARK_
steps: Create src/projects.json with a project whose background is 'GREEN_8'
Run src/projects-build
Assert dist/projects.json contains backgroundInactive.name='DARK_GREEN' with correct ansi and hex values

description: background without numeric suffix derives backgroundInactive by prepending DARK_
steps: Create src/projects.json with a project whose background is 'GREEN' (no numeric suffix)
Run src/projects-build
Assert dist/projects.json contains backgroundInactive.name='DARK_GREEN' with correct ansi and hex values

description: hideNameInPrompt true in src produces true in dist; absent in src produces false in dist
steps: Create src/projects.json with two projects: one with hideNameInPrompt:true, one without the key
Run src/projects-build
Assert dist/projects.json has hideNameInPrompt:true for the first project
Assert dist/projects.json has hideNameInPrompt:false for the second project

description: path without trailing slash in src is normalised with trailing slash in dist
steps: Create src/projects.json with a project whose path has no trailing slash
Run src/projects-build
Assert dist/projects.json contains the path with a trailing slash

description: dist/projects.zsh contains typeset -gA PROJECTS and dot-notation keys mirroring JSON paths
steps: Create a minimal src/projects.json with one project
Run src/projects-build
Assert dist/projects.zsh contains 'typeset -gA PROJECTS'
Assert dist/projects.zsh contains PROJECTS[projectname.background.name]
Assert dist/projects.zsh contains PROJECTS[projectname.background.ansi]
Assert dist/projects.zsh contains PROJECTS[projectname.background.hex]
Assert dist/projects.zsh contains PROJECTS[projectname.path]

description: src/projects.json is sorted alphabetically after the build runs
steps: Create src/projects.json with projects in non-alphabetical order (e.g. 'oroshi' before 'aberlaas')
Run src/projects-build
Assert src/projects.json now has 'aberlaas' before 'oroshi'

description: PROJECTS associative array is available in a new shell with correct values
steps: Ensure dist/projects.zsh exists (run src/projects-build if needed)
Start a new shell (source theming/index.zsh)
Assert $PROJECTS[oroshi.background.ansi] returns a non-empty integer value
Assert $PROJECTS[aberlaas.path] returns a tilde path ending with '/'

description: missing dist/projects.zsh is regenerated automatically on shell startup
steps: Delete dist/projects.zsh
Source theming/index.zsh
Assert dist/projects.zsh was recreated
Assert $PROJECTS is populated

description: PROJECT_* flat env vars are no longer exported after sourcing theming/index.zsh
steps: Source theming/index.zsh
Run: env | grep '^PROJECT_'
Assert output is empty (no PROJECT_* vars exported)

description: F.readJson returns a Lua table for a valid JSON file and nil for a missing file
steps: In NeoVim, call F.readJson('/path/to/valid.json') with a file containing {"key": "val"}
Assert the return value is a Lua table with result.key == 'val'
Call F.readJson('/nonexistent/path.json')
Assert the return value is nil

description: NeoVim statusline shows correct project background color from dist/projects.json
steps: Open a file inside a registered project directory in NeoVim
Assert the statusline background color matches the project's background.ansi value in dist/projects.json
Assert M.getProjectData() does not call 'project-key' or read PROJECT_* env vars

description: per-project data is cached — F.readJson is not called on every statusline render
steps: Open a file inside a registered project in NeoVim
Trigger multiple statusline renders
Assert F.readJson is called only once for the project (data cached in O.projects)

description: saving src/projects.json in NeoVim triggers src/projects-build and reloads dist files
steps: Open src/projects.json in NeoVim and save it
Assert src/projects-build ran asynchronously
Assert dist/projects.json is updated
Assert no manual reload prompt appeared (checktime ran automatically)

description: committing with src/projects.json staged runs src/projects-build and includes dist files
steps: Stage a change to src/projects.json
Run git commit
Assert src/projects-build was called by the pre-commit hook
Assert dist/projects.json and dist/projects.zsh are included in the commit

description: committing without src/projects.json staged does not run src/projects-build
steps: Stage a change to any file other than src/projects.json
Run git commit
Assert src/projects-build was NOT called

description: if src/projects-build fails the commit is aborted
steps: Stage a change to src/projects.json
Make src/projects-build exit with a non-zero status
Run git commit
Assert the commit was aborted

description: Kitty tab bar shows correct background, inactive background, foreground color and icon from dist/projects.json
steps: Ensure dist/projects.json exists with at least one project entry
Open a Kitty tab for a registered project
Assert the tab background color matches project.background.ansi
Assert an unfocused tab shows project.backgroundInactive.ansi
Assert the tab foreground and icon match the dist JSON values

description: initProjectList reads from dist/projects.json and no PROJECT_* flat key parsing remains
steps: Inspect projects.py — assert initProjectList() opens 'dist/projects.json'
Assert there are no references to keys ending in '_NAME', '_BACKGROUND', '_FOREGROUND', '_ICON' in projects.py
Assert color fields are accessed as project['background']['ansi'] style nested access

description: Kitty refresh beacon mechanism reloads project data on demand
steps: Trigger the Kitty refresh beacon (however it is invoked)
Assert initProjectList() is called again and picks up changes from dist/projects.json
